status: DRAFT — NOT RUN; design/materials only. No model calls have been made. No v5 numbers exist.
chief_cost_statement: The v5 can never say 'models pick the singular agreement the way humans do' — only that the construction shifts the within-model singular-agreement choice relative to a matched bare-plural control, in the direction the published AANN semantics predicts. This is a within-model contrast, not a human-comparison claim.
expected_agreement_key_provenance: EXPERT-STIPULATED. For an AANN measure-phrase subject the singular ('was') is the AANN-licensed agreement; for the bare-plural control the plural ('were') is the ordinary agreement. This is the design author's coding of the published AANN semantics (Solt 2007 unit-coercion; Dalrymple & King 2019; Bylinina & Nouwen 2018 — named as the analyses the stipulation rests on, NOT quoted; they are not in-repo). It is a scoring key, NOT a behavioral-human anchor.
headline_instrument: agreement (was/were forced choice), single contrast P(was|AANN) - P(was|control), unchanged from the v4 agreement arm
headline_statistic: agreement shift = P(was|AANN) - P(was|bare-plural control), per model
freshness_note: All adjectives here are HELD-OUT: none appears in the v3/v4 item set (beautiful, gruelling, glorious, miserable, restful, punishing, memorable, turbulent, sleepless, hectic, blissful, brutal, frantic, scenic, muddy, breathtaking, steep, lonely, treacherous, dazzling, relentless). Adjective x measure-noun combinations are fresh. Measure-noun classes are temporal + distance only (the 'genuine extents' the v3/v4 line kept); the count-noun diagnostic arm uses ordinary count nouns deliberately.

arm_field_map.agreement: held-out items; each has an 'agreement' sub-object with aann_frame, control_frame, agr_letter_was (MIRRORS the v4 agreement sub-structure exactly).
arm_field_map.diagnostic: count-noun controls; DESCRIPTIVE-ONLY, never verdict-bearing. Each has a 'diagnostic' sub-object with control_frame (bare count plural), agr_letter_was, expected = 'were' (a genuine count plural robustly takes plural).
arm_field_map.tier0: was/were manipulation check; clearly grammatical-vs-ungrammatical agreement pairs; >= threshold per model to confirm the model reads the was/were task at all.

items.aann: We endured an exhausting three days at the summit.
items.control_bare_plural: We endured three exhausting days at the summit.

agreement.aann_frame: An exhausting three days ___ what the climb demanded.
agreement.control_frame: Three exhausting days ___ what the climb demanded.
